title: Roasted Crispy Japanese Duck Legs Bao
description: Lazy Sunday Japanese dish featuring roasted, crispy duck legs served in a steamed bao with a side of beurre-monte sauce.
instructions: In a small bowl, whisk together soy sauce, sake, honey, rice vinegar, sugar, and 2 tbsp cornstarch.
Add 2 tbsp vegetable oil to a large bowl and whisk in the soy sauce mixture until combined.
Add the duck legs to the bowl and toss until they are well coated with the marinade.
Let the duck legs marinate for at least 30 minutes, or up to 2 hours in the refrigerator.
Line a baking sheet with aluminum foil and spray with vegetable oil.
Arrange the duck legs on the baking sheet in a single layer.
Roast the duck legs in the preheated oven for 25-30 minutes, or until they are browned and crispy on the outside.
While the duck legs are roasting, prepare the bao bun dough according to the package instructions.
Once the duck legs are done roasting, let them rest for 5 minutes before slicing them into individual portions.
Serve the duck legs in the bao buns with a side of beurre-monte sauce and garnished with scallions.
To make the beurre-monte sauce, combine 1 cup heavy cream, 2 tbsp unsalted butter, and 1 tsp soy sauce in a small saucepan over medium heat.
Whisk the mixture constantly until it comes to a simmer and thickens slightly.
Season the beurre-monte sauce with salt and pepper to taste.
